candidates.evidence_quote: Revenues increased 9.1% to $866.1 million for the fiscal quarter ended April 4, 2023 compared to $793.7 million for the comparable prior year period, primarily due to an increase in comparable restaurant sales
North Italia comparable sales increased approximately 9% from the first quarter of fiscal 2022
The increase from fiscal 2022 was primarily driven by an increase in average check of 4.7%
based on an increase of 10.4% in menu pricing, partially offset by 5.7% negative impact from mix
Average check variations are driven by menu price increases and/or changes in menu mix.
increased customer traffic of 1.0%
Sales through the off-premise channel comprised approximately 23% of our restaurant sales during the first quarter of fiscal 2023 as compared to 28% in the first quarter of fiscal 2022
The Cheesecake Factory average sales per restaurant operating week increased 6.3% to $239,679 in the first quarter of fiscal 2023
Total operating weeks at The Cheesecake Factory restaurants increased 1.2% to 2,737 in the first quarter of fiscal 2023
Our overall revenue growth is primarily driven by revenues from new restaurant openings and increases in comparable restaurant sales.
Preopening costs were $3.1 million and $1.8 million in the first quarters of fiscal 2023 and 2022, respectively.
We anticipate commodity inflation to be in the high-single digit range
This decrease was primarily due to menu pricing leverage in excess of wage rate inflation (1.0%)
a decline in group medical insurance costs due to lower claim activity (0.3%)
We have also encountered delays in opening new restaurants due to supply chain challenges and delays in permitting, construction, landlord readiness, and equipment availability.
In recent years, we have encountered delays in opening new restaurants due to supply chain challenges, as well as to longer lead times in obtaining licenses and permits.
Returns are affected by the cost to build restaurants, the level of revenues that each restaurant can deliver and our ability to maximize the profitability of restaurants
we recorded impairment of assets and lease terminations expense of $2.2 million primarily related to lease termination costs for one Grand Lux Cafe location
Our effective income tax rate was 12.5% and 13.8% for the first quarters of fiscal 2023 and 2022, respectively.
The decrease was primarily due to a higher proportion of employment credits in relation to income before income taxes
we anticipate total revenue for fiscal 2023 to be approximately $3.55 billion
we expect fiscal 2023 net income margin of approximately 4%
We anticipate approximately $165 million to $175 million in cash capital expenditures to support this level of unit development
Cash flows from operations increased by $31.5 million from the first quarter of fiscal 2022 primarily due to higher net income
We repurchased 0.3 million shares at a cost of $12.4 million during the first quarter of fiscal 2023
Common stock dividends of $13.2 million were paid in the first quarter of fiscal 2023.
could lead to further government mandates, including but not limited to capacity restrictions, shifts in consumer behavior, wage inflation, staffing challenges
we experienced increased restaurant staff absenteeism and temporary shifts in consumer behavior
augmenting our marketing programs including the anticipated nationwide launch of a guest rewards program
increases in minimum wages and benefit costs
foreign exchange rates, tariffs and cross border taxation
foreign exchange rates, tariffs and cross border taxation
rising interest rates, periods of heightened inflation and market instability

notes: Single event in chunk: 10-Q MD&A part 1 of 2 (accession 0001104659-23-058507, 2023-05-10); names coined only from this visible part.
fiscal_kpis is null on this chunk; no KPI rewrites.

Flagship brand 'The Cheesecake Factory' equals the company legal name (banned in driver_name), so its brand-level metrics map to company-wide names (same_store_sales, average_weekly_sales, restaurant_operating_weeks); North Italia is a distinct brand kept as its own driver per R9.

Skipped as vague/duplicative/instance-bound (16): menu_innovation, gift_card_program, third_party_delivery, bakery_operations, g_and_a_expenses, occupancy_expenses (no causal variance claims), 53rd-week fiscal calendar shift (period-bound name; effect captured via restaurant_operating_weeks), convertible_senior_notes, credit_facility_covenants (descriptive only), incentive_compensation_timing, adverse_weather, unemployment_rates, armed_conflicts_political_unrest, consumer_confidence, staffing_challenges, landlord_economic_health.
Quotes for tariff_policy and foreign_exchange_rates share one sentence naming both causal variables; emitted as separate drivers per R2.
